data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness criticism strictly beats self-consistency only when candidate base error exceeds 50% so that self-consistency's majority is wrong, while critic recall on the wrong majority exceeds (1 - precision) times the fraction of correct-majority questions the critic will false-flag.","mechanism": "Self-consistency computes the mode of k candidates; when base error > 0.5 the mode is the wrong answer. The critic acts as a binary classifier over candidate sets; it can add value only by vetoing the wrong majority while sparing the right one. Net benefit = recall_wrong * p_wrong_majority - FPR_right * p_right_majority, where FPR_right = 1 - precision on right-majority sets. Thus filtering strictly improves aggregate accuracy only in the intermediate regime: p_wrong_majority large enough and critic selectivity high enough that the first term dominates the second. The repository's measured null results (v4-pro hard: p_right_majority ~1, recall 1, precision 0.125) are the special case where there is nothing to fix and false flags are harmless only because the base majority was already right; at slightly higher base error the same precision would erode correct majorities and cause net loss.","scope": {"covers": ["intermediate base error 0.35-0.65", "critic recall on wrong-majority sets >= 0.5", "critic precision >= 0.5", "k=5 or k=7"], "excludes": ["base error < 0.2 (majority already right, filtering can only hurt)", "base error > 0.8 (majority robustly wrong, critic recall unlikely to rescue)", "perfect or near-perfect critic precision with near-zero recall"]},"forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment arm with base error 0.40-0.55 and critic precision >= 0.5 and recall >= 0.5 shows harness accuracy <= self-consistency accuracy on >= 60% of questions in that regime", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment arm with base error 0.40-0.55 and critic precision < 0.5 shows harness accuracy >= self-consistency accuracy on >= 50% of questions", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Across all intermediate-base-error arms, the measured quantity recall_wrong * p_wrong_majority - FPR_right * p_right_majority is negative while harness accuracy exceeds self-consistency", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}],"prose_notes": "The decisive experiment: construct ~120 questions across five base-error bins (0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.60, 0.75) by mixing difficulty tiers and prompting noise; generate k=5 candidates per question with one shared pool; run critic filter and raw majority vote; record per-question majority correctness, critic verdict, critic precision/recall stratified by whether the majority was right or wrong; compute net-fixed-minus-broke per bin. Expected signature: harness strictly beats SC in the 0.45-0.60 bin when critic precision >= 0.5, and harness loses to SC in the 0.15-0.30 bin when critic precision < 0.4 because false flags erode already-correct majorities. Token budget: 120 q * 5 cand * ~800 tokens generation + 120 q * ~600 tokens critic + ~3000 tokens analysis ~= 147k tokens, within 300k."}
